title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7306, ENTITLED ГўВЂВњAN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE PEOPLEГўВЂВ™S TELEVISION NETWORK, INCORPORATED, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, PROVIDING FOR ITS SOURCES OF FUNDING AND FOR OTHER PURPOSESГўВЂВќ

chunks.text:
H. No. 6703; 109 OG No. 21, 3716 (May 27, 2013); Philippine Star; Malaya, March 22, 2013

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10390, March 14, 2013 ]

AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7306, ENTITLED ГўВЂВњAN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE PEOPLEГўВЂВ™S TELEVISION NETWORK, INCORPORATED, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, PROVIDING FOR ITS SOURCES OF FUNDING AND FOR OTHER PURPOSESГўВЂВќ

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

TITLE I. ГўВЂВ“ GENERAL PROVISIONS

SECTION 1.

Title.

ГўВЂВ“ This Act shall be known as ГўВЂВњAn Act Revitalizing the PeopleГўВЂВ™s Television Network, IncorporatedГўВЂВќ.

TITLE II. ГўВЂВ“ CREATION AND DOMICILE

SEC. 3. Section 5 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 5.

Domicile.

ГўВЂВ“ The Network shall have its principal office and domicile in the Metropolitan Manila area. It may also establish offices, branches and/or stations in any part of the Philippines as its Board of Directors may see fit.

ГўВЂВњThe President, upon the recommendation of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources and its agencies particularly the Land Management Bureau and the National Mapping and Resource Information Authority, shall identify and appropriate alienable and disposable lands for the Network to establish and build its main broadcast center and major provincial stations.ГўВЂВќ

TITLE IV. ГўВЂВ“ POWERS AND FUNCTIONS

TITLE V. ГўВЂВ“ CORPORATE STRUCTURE

SEC. 6. Section 10 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 10.

The Board of Directors.

ГўВЂВ“ The corporate powers of the Network shall be exercised, all its business conducted and all its property controlled and held by the Board of Directors. It shall be composed of five (5) members who shall be appointed by the President of the Philippines from a shortlist prepared by the Governance Commission for Government-owned or -controlled corporations (GCG). The Board shall be made up of the following:

ГўВЂВњ(a) two (2) members from the government sector;

ГўВЂВњ(b) two (2) members from the private sector, one (1) of whom shall have at least ten (10) years of experience in the broadcast industry; and

ГўВЂВњ(c) one (1) member from the educational sector.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 7. Insert a new Section 10-A which shall read:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 10-A.

The Advisory Council.

ГўВЂВ“ Sixty (60) days from their appointment, the Board of Directors shall prepare a list of its recommended nominees for the members of the Advisory Council and which shall be submitted to the Secretary of the Presidential Communications Operations Office (PCOO) for consideration and approval. The nominees for Advisory Council shall be chosen from associations and organizations representing various stakeholders from different industries such as, but not limited to, cinema, culture and arts, print and broadcast. They shall serve a fixed term of one (1) year.

ГўВЂВњThe Advisory Council shall be composed of seven (7) members with at least five (5) years of wide experience and expertise in their respective fields of work. The Advisory Council shall propose and recommend policies relating to the NetworkГўВЂВ™s operation, programming, broadcasting, technical and creative production, development and management, which shall be subject to the approval of the Board of Directors.

ГўВЂВњThe members of the Advisory Council shall elect from among themselves a Chairperson.

ГўВЂВњThe members of the Advisory Council shall elect from among themselves a Chairperson.

ГўВЂВњThe members of the Advisory Council shall render voluntary service to the Network. No member shall be paid any honoraria, allowances and other personnel benefits.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 8. Section 12 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 12.

Term of the Members of the Board.

ГўВЂВ“ The term of office of each director shall be for one (1) year subject to reappointment by the President.

ГўВЂВњThe members shall elect from among themselves who will serve as Chairperson of the Board. The Board shall meet at least twice a month to discuss matters of policy in order to effectively carry out the provisions of this Act.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 9. Section 14 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 14.

The Network General Manager.

ГўВЂВ“ The members of the Board shall elect from among themselves a Network General Manager. He shall also be the Chief Operating Officer of the Network, who shall be responsible for the proper administration and management of the Network in accordance with the policies laid down by the Board. The General Manager shall be subject to the disciplinary powers of the Board and may be removed by the Board for cause.

ГўВЂВњIf the Network General Manager is absent, or is incapacitated by reason of illness or for any other cause, is temporarily unable to perform his duties, the Board of Directors may designate an officer-in-charge to act in his place and stead during such absence from duty.ГўВЂВќ

TITLE VI. ГўВЂВ“ PROGRAM STANDARDS

TITLE VII. ГўВЂВ“ FINANCIAL PROVISIONS

SEC. 12. Section 18 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 18.

Capitalization.

ГўВЂВ“ The authorized capital stock of the Network shall be Six billion pesos (P6,000,000,000.00) divided into six million (6,000,000) shares with par value of One thousand pesos (P1,000.00) per share, which shall be subscribed in full by the government.

ГўВЂВњOf the additional authorized capital of Five billion pesos (P5,000,000,000.00), Two billion pesos (P2,000,000,000.00) shall be taken from the proceeds of the privatization of Radio Philippines Network (RPN 9) and Intercontinental Broadcasting Corporation (IBC 13). The remaining Three billion pesos (P3,000,000,000.00) shall be appropriated under the General Appropriations Act.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 13. Section 19 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 19.

Revenue Generation.

ГўВЂВ“ The Network shall be allowed to generate hinds from advertising and airtime sales in accordance with the policies and rates set by the Board of Directors subject to existing laws. All fees or other revenues collected or received by the Network shall be retained by it and utilized solely for its operations and capital expenditure program.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 14. Insert a new Section 19-A which shall read:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 19-A.

Exemption from Republic Act No. 7656.

ГўВЂВ“ As provided in Republic Act No. 7656, the President, upon the recommendation by the Secretary of Finance, may adjust the percentage of the NetworkГўВЂВ™s annual net earnings to be remitted to the National Treasury:

Provided,

That the annual remaining unremitted net earnings be used solely for its operations and capital expenditures.ГўВЂВќ

TITLE IX. ГўВЂВ“ TAX EXEMPTIONS

TITLE IX. ГўВЂВ“ TAX EXEMPTIONS

SEC. 15. Insert a new Section 21-A which shall read:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 21-A. Gifts and donations of real and personal properties of all kinds to the Network shall be exempt from donorГўВЂВ™s tax and the same shall be considered as an allowable deduction from the gross income of the donor in accordance with the provisions of the National Internal Revenue Code of 1997, as amended:

Provided,

That the allowable deduction shall be equivalent to one hundred percent (100%) of the value of such donation:

Provided, further,

That the said donation shall be used exclusively for the production of educational, cultural and historical films and documentaries.

ГўВЂВњValuation of assistance, other than the money, shall be based on the acquisition cost of the property. Such valuation shall take into consideration the depreciated value of property in case said property has been used.ГўВЂВќ

TITLE X. ГўВЂВ“ EMPLOYEES OF THE NETWORK

TITLE X. ГўВЂВ“ EMPLOYEES OF THE NETWORK

SEC. 16. Section 22 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 22.

Civil Service Law Coverage for Employees.

ГўВЂВ“ The hiring, appointment, employment, promotion, disciplinary control, and other terms and conditions of the service of all employees of the Network, and such other matters affecting its employees shall be consistent with the provisions of the Civil Service law, rules and regulations:

Provided, however,

That positions considered as creative and artistic in nature shall he exempted from the qualification standards and civil service eligibility requirements.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 17. Section 23 of Republic Act No. 7306 is hereby amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 23.

Salary Scale for Officials and Employees.

ГўВЂВ“ Pursuant to the provisions of Republic Act No. 10149, the GCG shall develop a compensation and position classification system which shall apply to all officers and employees of the Network subject to the approval of the President of the Philippines.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 18.

Reorganization of the Network.

ГўВЂВ“ Ninety (90) days from the approval of this Act, the Board of Directors is hereby empowered to reorganize the Network to make it more effective and innovative. For this purpose, the Board may abolish or create offices, transfer functions, equipment, properties, records, and personnel, institute cost-cutting and other related measures to carry out the said objectives, subject to the provisions of Republic Act No. 10149, otherwise known as the ГўВЂВњGOCC Governance Act of 2011?:

Provided,

That any official or employee of the Network who may be affected by reason of the reorganization authorized herein shall be entitled to such benefits.

SEC. 19.

Separation and Retirement Benefits.

ГўВЂВ“ In the event an employee is separated from the Network by reason of reorganization, abolition, or creation of offices, or institution of cost-cutting and other similar measures, the employee shall be entitled to a separation benefit equivalent to one (1) month salary for every year of service in the government:

Provided,

That the separated or displaced employee has rendered at least one (1) year of service at the time of the effectivity of this Act.

TITLE XI. ГўВЂВ“ MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS

SEC. 20.

Implementing Rules and Regulations.

ГўВЂВ“ Within ninety (90) days from the effectivity of this Act, the Presidential Communications Operations Office (PCOO) shall draft, the implementing rules and regulations of this Act in consultation with the various stakeholders from government, and private sectors.

SEC. 21.

Amendments.

ГўВЂВ“ The provisions of this Act shall be subject to amendments, alteration or repeal by the Congress of the Philippines, when public interest so requires.

SEC. 22.

Separability Clause.

ГўВЂВ“ If any provision or part of this Act is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remainder shall not be affected thereby and shall continue to be in full force and effect.

SEC. 23.

Repealing Clause.

ГўВЂВ“ All laws, decrees, executive orders, administrative orders, rules and regulations, and other issuances or parts thereof inconsistent herewith are hereby repealed, amended or modified accordingly.

SEC. 24.

Effectivity Clause.

ГўВЂВ“ This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in two (2) national newspapers of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

(Sgd.)

JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

This Act which is a consolidation of Senate Bill No. 3316 and House Bill No. 6703 was finally passed by the Senate and the House of Representatives on January 21, 2013 and December 19, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.)

MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

(Sgd.)

EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

Approved:

MAR 14, 2013

(Sgd.)

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
